f: SQL Database vs Managed Instance vs SQL on VM
b: SQL DB: cloud-first single DBs. MI: near-full SQL Server compat (Agent, cross-DB), VNet-native — lift-and-shift. VM: full OS/engine control.
DTU: bundled blend, simple/small. vCore: independent compute/storage, Hybrid Benefit, reservations, serverless.
General Purpose: default, remote storage. Business Critical: local SSD, readable secondary, lowest latency. Hyperscale: 100+ TB, instant restores, fast replicas.
Auto-scales vCores and auto-pauses; pay storage only while paused — intermittent dev/test.
Many variable-usage databases share provisioned resources — multi-tenant SaaS cost optimization.
Strong → Bounded Staleness → Session (default; read-your-own-writes) → Consistent Prefix → Eventual.
Provisioned standard, autoscale (variable sustained), serverless (per-request; low/spiky traffic).
High cardinality, even distribution, matches dominant filters; avoid hot partitions; 20 GB logical partition cap.
Active-active writes in all regions, RPO≈0, conflict resolution policies; 99.999% SLA potential.
Ordered stream of container changes — event sourcing, materialized views, downstream triggers.
LRS (1 DC) → ZRS (3 zones) → GRS/RA-GRS (async pair region) → GZRS/RA-GZRS (zones + region; highest durability).
Hot → Cool (30d) → Cold (90d) → Archive (180d, offline, rehydration hours). Lifecycle policies auto-tier/expire.
WORM: time-based retention or legal hold — compliance (SEC 17a-4) and ransomware defense.
Blob + hierarchical namespace: directories, POSIX ACLs — the data-lake foundation.
On-prem Windows servers become hot caches of Azure Files with cloud tiering.
OLTP relational: SQL. Global NoSQL: Cosmos. Objects/lake: Blob/ADLS. SMB shares: Files. Cache: Redis. Telemetry analytics: ADX.
RTO = max downtime. RPO = max data loss. Drive every BC design decision from these two numbers per workload tier.
Backup = point-in-time recovery (corruption/ransomware/deletion). Replication = infrastructure loss. Replication copies corruption too — need both.
Recovery Services vault: VMs, SQL-in-VM, Files, MARS. Backup vault: blobs, disks, PostgreSQL.
Soft delete (14+ days), immutable vaults, multi-user authorization for destructive changes.
Continuous VM replication + recovery plans (ordered tiers, scripts) + test failover in isolated networks. RPO seconds-minutes.
Cross-region group failover with stable RW/RO listener endpoints — apps reconnect without config changes.
PITR 1–35 days; long-term retention (LTR) weekly/monthly/yearly up to 10 years; geo-restore from geo-backups.
Single VM 99.9% → availability set 99.95% → zones 99.99% → multi-region (paired) for regional disasters.
Passive: priority routing, cheaper, higher RTO. Active: both serve traffic; needs multi-write or partitioned data.
Customer-initiated with (RA-)GRS promotes secondary; data after Last Sync Time is lost (async replication).
Managed fault injection (zone down, CPU stress, network latency) to validate resilience assumptions.
Metrics: numeric time-series, near-real-time alerts. Logs: Log Analytics workspace, KQL, richer/slower.
APM: distributed tracing, Application Map, live metrics, availability tests, smart detection; workspace-based.
Metric (fast, platform metrics), log/scheduled-query (KQL), activity log, smart detection → all fire action groups.
Reusable receiver sets: email/SMS/push/webhook/Function/Logic App/ITSM. Alert processing rules suppress/route at scale.
Log Analytics (analyze), Storage (cheap archive), Event Hubs (external SIEM/stream).
Centralize per environment/region for correlation; resource-context/table RBAC isolates teams; split only for sovereignty/chargeback.
Retention tuning, long-term/archive tiers, daily caps, Basic/Auxiliary table plans, App Insights sampling.
Service Health: Azure platform incidents/maintenance (alertable). Resource Health: your resource's availability state.
Defender: CSPM (secure score) + workload protection. Sentinel: SIEM/SOAR — cross-source correlation, hunting, playbooks.
NSG/VNet flow logs, connection monitor, IP flow verify, packet capture — network diagnostics toolkit.
OS control: VM/VMSS. Web PaaS: App Service. Event code: Functions. Microservices sans K8s: ACA. Full K8s: AKS. One container: ACI. HPC: Batch.
99.9% single (premium SSD) / 99.95% availability set / 99.99% across zones.
Up to ~90% off, evictable with 30s notice — batch, CI, stateless only.
Consumption (scale-to-zero, cold starts) / Flex Consumption / Premium (pre-warmed, VNet, unlimited) / Dedicated plan.
Chaining, fan-out/fan-in, async HTTP APIs, monitor, human interaction, saga orchestration.
Private cluster, Entra workload identity for pods, Entra+K8s RBAC, Defender for Containers, GitOps (Flux).
Reservations (deepest, locked family/region), savings plans (flexible $/hr commit), Hybrid Benefit (licenses), Spot (interruptible).
Rehost (lift-shift) · Refactor (minor PaaS changes) · Rearchitect (microservices) · Rebuild (cloud-native rewrite) · Replace (SaaS).
Discovery, dependency mapping, right-size + cost assessment, then migration of servers/DBs/web apps.
Online (minimal downtime, continuous sync) or offline migrations to Azure SQL family; DMA assesses compatibility first.
Encrypted appliance for offline TB–PB transfers when bandwidth is impractical.
Integration runtime installed on-prem to reach private/on-prem data sources from Data Factory pipelines.
ETL orchestration: ADF. Unified SaaS analytics: Fabric. Spark/ML: Databricks. Streams SQL: Stream Analytics. Telemetry KQL: ADX. Low-code workflows: Logic Apps.
Soft delete (default, recoverable) + purge protection (no permanent delete until retention ends — required for CMK).
Standard: software keys. Premium: shared HSM-backed. Managed HSM: dedicated single-tenant FIPS 140-2 Level 3 pool.
Prefer Azure RBAC data roles (Secrets User, Crypto User…) over legacy access policies; private endpoints + disabled public access.
Central non-secret settings + feature flags, with Key Vault references for secrets — pairs with safe deployment/feature rings.
Projects on-prem/multicloud servers, K8s, and data services into ARM — Policy, RBAC, tags, Monitor, Defender everywhere.
Bicep: Azure-native, day-0 ARM, no state file. Terraform: multicloud, state-managed, huge ecosystem. Both exam-valid IaC.
Manage deployments as units: deny settings block out-of-band edits; deleted-from-template resources cleaned up. Blueprints successor.
OIDC workload identity federation from GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps — zero stored cloud secrets.
Azure Load Testing (JMeter/Locust at scale) + Chaos Studio (fault injection) wired into CI/CD gates.
